title: 楚辞 · Songs of Chu
desc: The shamanistic southern voice that gave Chinese literature its second pole — visionary, romantic, and inconsolably exiled.

title: 昆曲 · Kun Opera
desc: The oldest surviving form of Chinese opera — refined to such perfection over four centuries that it nearly disappeared from its own beauty.

title: 鲁迅 · Lu Xun
desc: The writer who looked at traditional Chinese society and saw cannibalism — and the complicated, unresolvable legacy he left behind.

title: 诗经 · Book of Songs
desc: China oldest poetry anthology — three hundred poems that Confucius said no educated person could afford to ignore.

title: 水墨画 · Ink-Wash Painting
desc: Where the brush meets philosophy — five tones of ink, the art of leaving space, and landscapes that are not about landscape at all.

title: 宋词 · Song Dynasty Lyric
desc: The poem that sings — fixed tune patterns, named meters, and three masters who defined what the Chinese lyric could feel.

title: 文房四宝 · Four Treasures of the Study
desc: Brush, ink, inkstone, and paper — the four instruments through which a scholar thought, wrote, painted, and demonstrated who he was.

title: 篆刻 · Seal Carving
desc: The art of the name — bronze to stone, dynasty to dynasty, two schools of carvers, and the small red square that authenticates everything.

title: 不 · no, not, negation
desc: The great negator — the most used character in Mandarin and the foundation of all denial.

title: 大 · big, great, large
desc: Big, great, large — a person with arms wide open, the original measure of magnitude.

title: 佛 · Buddha, the awakened
desc: The character that carried an entire worldview across the Silk Road — etymology, Buddhism, the 佛-family of compounds.

title: 好 · good; to like
desc: Good, well, to like — and the tone split that doubles its expressive range.

title: 角 · corner, horn, angle
desc: Corner, horn, angle — the character that names this field guide.

title: 落 · to fall, to settle, to land
desc: Falling, settling, and the beauty of things arriving at their place.

title: 木 · tree, wood
desc: A tree seen whole: trunk, branches, roots. The pictograph that became a radical, a wuxing element, and the building block of 林, 森, 本, and 末.

title: 书 · book, writing, letter
desc: Book, writing, calligraphy — the character that holds civilization together.

title: 水 · water
desc: The first of the five elements, the radical that runs through hundreds of compounds, and the Daoist image of how to act in the world.

title: 说 · to speak, to say, to explain
desc: To speak, say, explain, and persuade — the character at the center of all communication.

title: 王 · king
desc: Three horizontals joined by one vertical — the ruler who connects heaven, earth, and humanity. Also the most common Chinese surname, and a radical that is secretly 玉 (jade) in disguise.

title: 屋 · room, house, dwelling
desc: Room, dwelling, and the human need for shelter — the character at home.

title: 要 · to want, must, essential
desc: Want, need, must, and will — the grammar engine disguised as a single character.

title: 再 · again, then, further
desc: Again, one more time, and the subtle art of "and then" — the character that sequences and repeats.

title: 在 · to be at, located, in progress
desc: Location, existence, and the progressive aspect — the character that puts things in place and actions in motion.

title: 画蛇添足 · Drawing Legs on a Snake
desc: He finished first, so he kept drawing — and lost the prize. The idiom for ruining a good thing by overdoing it.

title: 刻舟求剑 · Carving the Boat to Find the Sword
desc: He marked the boat where his sword fell into the river. When the boat reached the shore, he dived where the mark was.

title: 塞翁失马 · The Old Man at the Frontier Loses His Horse
desc: His horse ran off. His neighbours grieved. He shrugged. The Daoist parable that became China's word for the unknowability of fortune.

title: 守株待兔 · Guarding the Stump, Waiting for Rabbits
desc: A rabbit ran headlong into a tree stump. The farmer dropped his plough and watched the stump for the rest of his life.

title: 愚公移山 · The Foolish Old Man Moves the Mountains
desc: Two mountains blocked his door. He and his sons began to move them, one basket at a time. A mocker laughed. He said: when I die my sons will continue; when they die, their sons.

title: 八大菜系 · Eight Great Cuisines
desc: How geography, climate, and dynasty shaped eight distinct schools of Chinese cooking.

title: 川菜 · Sichuan Cuisine
desc: The mala kitchen — how a landlocked basin built the most complex spice vocabulary in the world.

title: 豆腐 · Tofu
desc: Two thousand years of soybeans and water: the food that fed a civilization without meat.

title: 火锅 · Hotpot
desc: The communal pot at the center of the table: a social institution disguised as a meal.

title: 鲁菜 · Shandong Cuisine
desc: The oldest school — how the home province of Confucius fed the imperial court for two thousand years.

title: 面条 · Noodles
desc: Four thousand years of wheat in water — noodle cultures from Lanzhou to Suzhou.

title: 粤菜 · Cantonese Cuisine
desc: Freshness as philosophy: the cooking tradition that traveled the world and stayed true to itself.

title: 茶文化 · Tea Culture
desc: Tea as civilization — the way a leaf became a philosophical practice, a diplomatic currency, and the shape of daily life across two millennia.

title: 汉服 · Traditional Han Dress
desc: Ancient silhouettes worn in modern streets — the revival of Han dynasty clothing and the identity politics it carries.

title: 红包 · Red Envelopes
desc: Money wrapped in red — the gift that is never just money, and the ritual that binds generations, occasions, and now hundreds of millions of WeChat users.

title: 清明节 · Tomb-Sweeping Day
desc: The day the living visit the dead — a spring festival of grief, gratitude, and continuity across generations.

title: 太极 · Tai Chi
desc: The slow-motion practice in the park at dawn — its philosophy is older than the movements, and the movements carry the whole philosophy.

title: 中秋节 · Mid-Autumn Festival
desc: The moon at its fullest, the family reunited — the festival that turns a celestial event into a meditation on longing and belonging.

title: 称呼 · Forms of Address
desc: In Chinese, how you address someone is not a formality — it is a precise statement of how you understand the relationship between you.

title: 红白喜事 · Weddings & Funerals
desc: The phrase itself tells the story: red and white, marriage and death, both require the same thing — the whole community showing up.

title: 敬酒 · Toasting Culture
desc: The Chinese toast is not a punctuation mark at the end of a speech — it is a social instrument with its own grammar, hierarchy, and rules of engagement.

title: 迷信 · Superstitions
desc: Superstition in China is not a holdover from ignorance — it is a living system that coexists comfortably with education, modernity, and pragmatism.

title: 请客吃饭 · Treating to a Meal
desc: The Chinese banquet is not a meal with social dimensions — it is a social institution that happens to involve food.

title: 数字 · Lucky & Unlucky Numbers
desc: In China, numbers are not neutral — they carry sound, and sound carries meaning, and meaning can determine whether you close the deal or jinx the wedding.

title: 送礼 · Gift-Giving
desc: Gift-giving in China is less about generosity than about maintenance — a technology for building and sustaining the relationships that make life work.

title: 八大菜系 · Cuisines as Geography
desc: Read the Eight Great Cuisines as a geographic document — what the land, climate, and trade routes of each region produced at the table, and what the table tells you about the land.

title: 方言 · The Dialect Map
desc: Chinese is not one language but a family — seven major dialect groups whose speakers cannot understand one another, united by a shared written script and divided by a history of geography and migration.

title: 黄河 · Yellow River
desc: The cradle of Chinese civilization — a river that floods, destroys, and nourishes in equal measure, its loess-yellow waters shaping every dynasty that tried to master it.

title: 上海 · Shanghai
desc: From fishing village to the most cosmopolitan city in Asia — a port that absorbed empires, reinvented itself three times, and has never quite decided whether it belongs to China or the world.

title: 香港 · Hong Kong
desc: The entrepôt that became a world city — a harbor geography that made it the gateway between East and West, and a Cantonese identity that survived colonial rule and asks harder questions under reunification.

title: 新疆 · Xinjiang
desc: China's largest province. A vast desert interior crossed by the Silk Road, home to Uyghur civilization, bounded by the Tianshan mountains, and at the center of contested questions about identity and governance.

title: 西藏 · Tibet
desc: The Roof of the World — a plateau so high it generates its own weather, a civilization shaped by altitude, and a Buddhism that grew from the landscape as much as from doctrine.

title: 而且 · moreover, and furthermore
desc: Additive conjunction that layers information — not just "and" but "and on top of that."

title: 过 · the experiential aspect marker
desc: The particle that says 'I have ever done this' — not when, not how often, just that it once happened. The most-confused pair with 了.

title: 起来 · directional complement — upward, inward, beginning
desc: The most versatile directional complement in Mandarin — three distinct grammatical lives.

title: 如果…就 · if … then
desc: The foundational conditional frame — the engine of hypothetical reasoning in Mandarin.

title: 虽然…但是 · although … but
desc: The concession frame — granting the premise but pivoting to the real point.

title: 越来越 · more and more; increasingly
desc: The degree escalation pattern — expressing a quality that grows continuously over time.

title: 与其…不如 · rather than … it would be better to
desc: Comparative preference construction — the most elegant way to argue for one choice over another.

title: 本草纲目 · The Great Pharmacopeia
desc: Li Shizhen spent 27 years cataloguing 1,892 drugs — and produced one of the greatest works of natural science in any civilization.

title: 风水 · Geomancy as Proto-Science
desc: Before the terms ecology, hydrology, and orientation science existed, the fengshui master was reading the same landscape — just with a different vocabulary.

title: 二十四节气 · 24 Solar Terms
desc: The sun moves 15 degrees and the world changes — a precision agricultural calendar that doubled as a medical manual and is now UNESCO heritage.

title: 历法 · The Lunisolar Calendar
desc: Neither purely solar nor purely lunar, the Chinese calendar has kept farmers, physicians, and dynasties synchronized with the cosmos for three thousand years.

title: 算盘 · The Abacus
desc: Before the electronic calculator, a bead frame and trained fingers could outrun early computers on everyday arithmetic.

title: 星宿 · Chinese Constellations
desc: China mapped the entire sky independently of Greece — 1,464 stars, 28 lunar mansions, and four mythological guardians that divided the cosmos long before Ptolemy.

title: 针灸 · Acupuncture
desc: Needles, meridians, and the ancient practice that resists simple verdicts from modern science.

title: 笔记 · notes, jottings
desc: Notes, jottings, and the Chinese literary tradition of the brush-record.

title: 客气 · politeness, ceremony
desc: The polite manners owed to guests — and the gentle dance of refusal, insistence, and ritual modesty that runs through every Chinese social interaction.

title: 命运 · fate, destiny
desc: Fate, destiny, and the tension between cosmic order and human will.

title: 书法 · Calligraphy
desc: The art of the brush — where writing becomes the most personal expression of character.

title: 四大名著 · Four Great Classical Novels
desc: The four novels every educated Chinese person has read — the canonical stories of war, outlaws, pilgrims, and dreams.

title: 唐诗 · Tang Poetry
desc: Li Bai, Du Fu, and the golden age of regulated verse.

title: 道 · way, path
desc: The word that gave Daoism its name — and underlies every Chinese 'ism'.

title: 感 · to feel, to resonate
desc: Feeling, perception, gratitude, and the cosmology of sympathetic resonance.

title: 家 · home, family
desc: House, family, school of thought, nation (国家) — the fractal of belonging.

title: 气 · breath, vital energy
desc: From weather to anger to the cosmic force underlying TCM and martial arts.

title: 人 · person, humanity
desc: The character for human being, and its echo in 仁 rén — Confucian benevolence.

title: 是 · to be
desc: The copula, the cleft construction, classical 是非 right-and-wrong.

title: 天 · sky, heaven, day
desc: Heaven as moral authority, weather, time, and the ground of 天命 the Mandate of Heaven.

title: 心 · heart, mind
desc: The radical at the root of all feeling — and the Chinese conception of thought itself.

title: 有 · to have, to exist
desc: Possession, existence, and the have/be distinction that reshapes grammar.

title: 字 · character, written word
desc: The character that names itself — and the whole system of 汉字.

title: 矛盾 · Contradiction
desc: The spear that can pierce anything meets the shield that can stop anything — the story behind China's word for contradiction.

title: 叶公好龙 · Lord Ye Loves Dragons
desc: He decorated his palace with dragons — until a real one appeared. The idiom for loving what you only love in the abstract.

title: 欲速则不达 · Haste Does Not Bring Success
desc: Speed desired, arrival missed — Confucius on why rushing produces the opposite of what you intend.

title: 白酒 · Baijiu
desc: China's fiery grain spirit — the most consumed distilled liquor in the world by volume, and what it means to drink together.

title: 茶 · Tea
desc: Six tea categories, production, and the cup that connects China to the world.

title: 饺子 · Dumplings
desc: Folded, boiled, and shared — the dumpling as ritual food, family labor, and cultural marker.

title: 春节 · Spring Festival
desc: Lunar New Year. The largest annual human migration and the most important date on the Chinese calendar.

title: 京剧 · Peking Opera
desc: China's national theatrical art — acrobatics, painted faces, falsetto voices, and two centuries of stylized storytelling.

title: 生肖 · Chinese Zodiac
desc: The twelve animals, what they mean, and why your birth year still shapes how people see you.

title: 筷子 · Chopsticks
desc: The rules, the taboos, and what not to do — the etiquette that marks insider from outsider at the Chinese table.

title: 姓名 · Names
desc: Family name first, given name second — the structure, meaning-making, and social logic of Chinese personal names.

title: 颜色 · Colors and Luck
desc: Red for joy, white for death, green hats and yellow emperors — the symbolic weight of color in Chinese daily life.

title: 北京 · Beijing
desc: Capital geography — the hutong grid, the Forbidden City, and the northern axis that has organized Chinese power for seven centuries.

title: 长江 · The Yangtze River
desc: The longest river in Asia — the southern spine of Chinese civilization, the Three Gorges, and the river that divides north from south.

title: 台湾 · Taiwan
desc: An island with multiple names, multiple claims, and the most consequential unresolved question in East Asian geopolitics.

title: 把 · the disposal construction
desc: Moving the object before the verb — when and why.

title: 被 · the passive
desc: Agentive passive, unmarked passive, and the adversarial connotation.

title: 的 · 得 · 地 · the three de's
desc: Attribution, result complement, and adverbial marker — distinguished once and for all.

title: 来 · 去 · directional complements
desc: 上来, 下去, 出去, 进来 — the motion complement system mapped.

title: 会 · 能 · 可以 · the three 'can's
desc: Learned ability vs. situational capacity vs. permission — not interchangeable.

title: 就 vs. 才 · sooner vs. later
desc: The timing and emphasis pair — speaker attitude encoded in one syllable.

title: 了 · the perfective particle
desc: Perfective aspect vs. change-of-state — the single most-confused particle.

title: 连…都/也 · even X
desc: The intensifier construction — raising the stakes of a claim.

title: 量词 · measure words
desc: 个, 只, 条, 本, 张 — the classifier taxonomy and the logic behind it.

title: 是…的 · the cleft construction
desc: Focus and past-detail marking — the construction that trips every learner.

title: 汉朝 · The Han Dynasty
desc: Why Chinese call themselves 汉人 — the dynasty that defined the civilization.

title: 明朝 · The Ming Dynasty
desc: The Great Wall rebuilt, Zheng He's voyages, and the inward turn.

title: 秦始皇 · The First Emperor
desc: Unification, standardization, and the terror that made China.

title: 清朝 · The Qing Dynasty
desc: Manchu rule, the long peace, and the slow unraveling before 1911.

title: 宋朝 · The Song Dynasty
desc: Economic revolution, printing, compass — the dynasty modernity missed.

title: 唐朝 · The Tang Dynasty
desc: The cosmopolitan golden age — poetry, Buddhism, and the Silk Road.

title: 文化大革命 · The Cultural Revolution
desc: 1966–76: Red Guards, struggle sessions, and the destruction of the past.

title: 夏商周 · The Three Dynasties
desc: The legendary and early historical origins of Chinese civilization.

title: 辛亥革命 · The 1911 Revolution
desc: The end of empire, Sun Yat-sen, and the birth of the Republic.

title: 元朝 · The Yuan Dynasty
desc: Kublai Khan, Mongol rule, and China as the center of a world empire.

title: 法家 · Legalism
desc: Han Feizi, statecraft, and the realpolitik that unified China.

title: 孔子 · Confucius
desc: His life, the Analects 论语, and the civilization he shaped.

title: 老子 · Laozi & the Daodejing
desc: The 81 chapters, wu wei 无为, and the paradox at the heart of Daoist thought.

title: 理学 · Neo-Confucianism
desc: Zhu Xi, the Great Ultimate 太极, and the Song-Ming synthesis.

title: 孟子 · Mencius
desc: Human nature as inherently good — the sprouts of virtue.

title: 墨家 · Mohism
desc: Universal love, anti-war, and the utilitarian logic China nearly chose.

title: 心学 · Wang Yangming
desc: Innate knowing 良知 and the unity of knowledge and action.

title: 荀子 · Xunzi
desc: Human nature as bad — ritual and education as correction.

title: 阴阳五行 · Yin-Yang and the Five Phases
desc: The cosmological engine of classical thought — correspondence, resonance, and transformation.

title: 庄子 · Zhuangzi
desc: The butterfly dream, Cook Ding, and Daoist freedom from convention.

title: 禅宗 · Chán Buddhism
desc: The meditative school that became Japanese Zen — sudden enlightenment and the patriarchs.

title: 道教 · Daoism
desc: Daoism as organized religion — distinct from Daoist philosophy.

title: 佛教 · Buddhism in China
desc: Two thousand years of transmission, transformation, and sinicization.

title: 净土宗 · Pure Land
desc: The most widely practiced Buddhism in China — faith, Amitabha, and the Western Paradise.

title: 妈祖 · the Sea Goddess
desc: Coastal goddess of sailors, fishermen, and the overseas Chinese diaspora.

title: 民间信仰 · Folk Religion
desc: Kitchen gods, city gods, and the living popular religion beneath Buddhism and Daoism.

title: 儒家 · Confucianism
desc: The school of the ru — ritual, hierarchy, and moral cultivation as state religion.

title: 萨满教 · Shamanism
desc: Manchu, Mongol, and northern indigenous shamanic traditions.

title: 伊斯兰教 · Islam in China
desc: Hui Muslims, Uyghur traditions, and 1400 years of Islamic presence.

title: 祖先崇拜 · Ancestor Veneration
desc: The deep continuity — tablets, offerings, and the living debt to the dead.

title: 丝绸 · Silk
desc: The thread that built the first global trade network — Chinese silk, the secret of sericulture, and the road that carried it west.

title: 四大发明 · Four Great Inventions
desc: Compass, gunpowder, paper, and printing — the Chinese technologies that remade the medieval world.

title: 中医 · Traditional Chinese Medicine
desc: Qi, yin-yang, the five phases, and a medical system that has treated a fifth of humanity for two thousand years.

title: 东西 · things
desc: East-west as the word for 'stuff' — a window into directional cosmology.

title: 风水 · wind and water
desc: Geomancy, qi flow, and the cosmological logic of favorable siting.

title: 功夫 · effort, skill over time
desc: Not just martial arts — the original meaning is mastery earned through time.

title: 关系 · relationship, connections
desc: The untranslatable social fabric — relational leverage, it matters.

title: 江湖 · rivers and lakes
desc: The outsider world of wanderers, martial artists, and street wisdom.

title: 面子 · face, honor
desc: Giving, losing, and saving face — the social grammar of dignity.

title: 天下 · all under heaven
desc: The pre-nation-state Chinese political imagination — the civilized world.

title: 阴阳 · yin and yang
desc: The binary engine of all classical Chinese thought.

title: 意思 · meaning, intention
desc: Meaning, interest, charm, and the gift-giving idiom 意思意思.

title: 缘分 · karmic connection
desc: Buddhist-rooted fate that ties strangers, lovers, and old friends.
